query: Pennen: One quiet handwritten page a day. No feed, no AI. complaints OR frustrating OR broken

results.title: Pennen — A calm, private handwritten journal for iPad
results.content:
Pennen. It's handwriting-first rather than typing-first, has no feed or streak gamification, keeps everything in your own iCloud, and offers a one-time lifetime purchase. Day One remains the better choice for typed, multimedia, cross-platform journaling — the fair comparison.

### What do I need to run Pennen?

An iPad running iPadOS 26 or later. Pennen is an iPad-only app made for Apple Pencil.

Pennen - One quiet handwritten page a day. No feed, no AI. | Product Hunt [...] Pennen app icon

The handwritten journal for iPad

# Write by hand. Stay present.

A private page for every day, written with Apple Pencil on paper-like rules. No feed, no streaks, no AI reading your entries — just you and the page.

Download on the App Store

Made for iPad · iPadOS 26+ · Apple Pencil

From an independent maker · no investors, no ads, no one reading your pages

Pennen on iPad mini — a real handwritten journal page: 'Day one with Juno', written in ink with an emoji sticker

## A year you can hold.

One page a day becomes 365 pages a year — the day the puppy came home, the last day at the old job, the ordinary Tuesday you'd otherwise lose. Past pages seal and become read-only: what's written is written. A few days missed? It doesn't matter. Nothing scolds you here. [...] No ads · no tracking · your pages stay in your own iCloud

## From the notebook.

Honest guides and fair comparisons — the research behind the app.

All guides →

## Questions, answered.

### What is the best handwriting journal app for iPad?

If you want a dedicated daily journal rather than a general note-taking app, Pennen is the best handwriting-first journal for iPad: one dated page per day, written with Apple Pencil, stored only on your iPad and in your own iCloud — no account, no AI, no streaks. Apple's free Journal app is the best no-cost option, and GoodNotes or Notability are better if you want a general notebook rather than a daily journal.

### Why write by hand instead of typing?

results.title: A.I.: This was once essential to so many writers. Now it's vanishing across the internet.
results.content:
That was three years ago. Over the past 12 months, every one of those communities went quiet. Not all at once. The tone shifted first. Celebrations became complaints. The Slacks that used to be full of someone’s article going viral or a pitch getting accepted became places where people vented about A.I. slop flooding the platforms we wrote for. One news platform I’d been writing on allowed writers to use A.I. as long as they disclosed it. It soon filled up with so much generated content that the real articles were buried. But the human writing was easy to spot: It actually made sense. [...] # The Quiet Collapse of the Internet’s Writing Rooms

## For years, writers built communities online—trading drafts, advice, and friendship. Then A.I. arrived, and those spaces began to empty out.

A group of people with laptops around a table in a coffee shop but most of the people are fading and pixelated. 

Sign up for the Slatest to get the most insightful analysis, criticism, and advice out there, delivered to your inbox daily. [...] We designed writing courses together, ran group calls, built more communities. We had Zoom sessions where everyone kept their camera on, but nobody talked, just wrote in companionable silence. I loved it when someone coughed or a phone rang. When Kelly visited Australia, we had dinner, and it felt like catching up with someone I’d known for decades.

As my profile grew, I was invited to more writing groups, including a private one for some of the biggest freelance writers online. I stayed silent in that group for days, convinced someone would realize I was an impostor. Eventually, I typed something and hit send. Nobody kicked me out. Apparently, I had them fooled.

results.title: Curvapen
results.content:
Writing and drawing can get hard for reasons most people never see.

A signature.A note.A sketch.A birthday card.A quiet moment with paper.

The things that used to feel simple can suddenly feel frustrating, slow, awkward, or emotional.

## CurvaPen helps make writing and drawing feel:

 👁️ easier to see
 🤲 easier to guide
 ✨ easier to do your way

## Made for people dealing with:

 🧠 stroke
 💚 Parkinson’s
 🤲 arthritis
 ✨ tremors
 💪 hand weakness
 🔄 mobility changes
 ↖️ left-handed writing
 ✍️ overhand writing

## Why people choose CurvaPen

 👁️ Helps open the view of the page
 🤲 Supports a more natural hand angle
 ✍️ Works well for overhand writing
 ↖️ Helpful for many left-handers
 🌿 Feels less restrictive than traditional pens
 🎨 Makes writing and drawing feel more possible [...] image.png

# For Hands That Need a Different Way to Write & Draw

## See the page more clearly. Hold the pen more naturally. Write or draw again with more freedom, comfort, and joy.

Watch Demo

## Writing and drawing can get hard for reasons most people never see.

 A signature
 A note

The things that used to feel simple can suddenly feel frustrating, slow, awkward, or emotional.

🤲 easier to guide

✨ easier to do your way

# Made for people dealing with:

🧠 stroke

💚 Parkinson’s

🤲 arthritis

✨ tremors

💪 hand weakness

🔄 mobility changes

↖️ left-handed writing

✍️ overhand writing

# Why people choose CurvaPen [...] # WHY CURVAPEN FEELS DIFFERENT

## Why CurvaPen Feels Different

Traditional pens do not work well for everyone.

They can:

 block your view of the page
 force awkward hand angles
 make overhand writing harder
 make drawing feel stiff or frustrating
 turn a simple act into a daily fight

CurvaPen was created for people who need a different way to write and draw.

Not a miracle.Not a gimmick.A thoughtful tool designed to make writing and drawing feel more visible, more natural, and more possible.

## Its patented curved design helps:

 👁️ open the view of the page
 🤲 support more natural hand positions
 ✍️ work better with the way many people already write or draw
 🌿 make standard pens feel less limiting

results.title: The Author’s Guide to AI with Joanna Penn - Author Media
results.content:
Inside the text box, there’s a plus button with an agent dropdown. I can upload spreadsheets and ask it to reformat them into one master sheet, generate graphs, and so on. I used to pay someone to do that, but it was out of my control, took extra time, and was quite frustrating. Now I’m doing it myself with ChatGPT as my coworker.

## How do I know which tasks to use AI for?

Joanna: When considering what tools and tasks AI can help with in your author business, think first about what you do and don’t enjoy. Many authors don’t enjoy marketing or data analysis, and those are great starting points for using AI. [...] Check out her webinars. It’s cheaper if you become a patron of her podcast first. Pro tip: if you’re already supporting Novel Marketing on Patreon, it’s easy to add hers too and save money.

Keep that attitude of play. Don’t let dystopian films rob you of the fun. There’s so much to explore and so much to play with.

If you want more AI coverage, we share news on Author Update, and Joanna covers it at the start of each of her podcast episodes.

## Connect with Joanna

## Featured Patrons

J.D. Rempel, author of Write with the Lord: 40 Quick Quiet Times for Authors

This game-changing devotional offers writers two-minute spiritual power-ups that transform ordinary writing sessions into divine collaborations, reigniting your creative purpose while deepening your relationship with God. [...] If you’re nervous about AI having access to your book, pay the $20 and uncheck the box. I think it’s even unchecked by default now, though they may change the defaults in the future. As a paying customer, you control whether your data is shared.

Beyond privacy, you also get better models. ChatGPT hides all its different models behind the scenes, but you get the stronger ones if you pay. People who complain that AI produces rubbish are usually just using the free version. The paid version is better.

Joanna: Yes, and I think most people on the $20 plan can use the dropdown and change models. They actually brought that back because people wanted their old models for different tasks.
